name: surge_obstetrics_false_negative_covid_screen_admissions
premise: Chief medical officer Casey Marsh must decide whether to admit laboring patients to postpartum wards with rapid antigen screens that miss ten percent of symptomatic COVID during a variant wave. Infection control nurse Orin Alvarez demands PCR or single-room negative pressure that the hospital lacks. Diverting all positives stalls inductions for preeclampsia. Universal PCR backlog is eighteen hours. Trust in maternity is fragile after last year's outbreak.

agents.name: Casey Marsh
agents.identity: I am the chief medical officer balancing obstetric urgency, newborn safety, and infection control during respiratory surges.
agents.goal: I want mothers delivered safely without seeding a postpartum outbreak from false-negative screens.

agents.name: Orin Alvarez
agents.identity: I am the infection control nurse who triages PPE, isolation rooms, and cluster investigations when postpartum COVID slips through.
agents.goal: I want PCR confirmation or cohorting in pressure-capable units before mingling newborns, even if that delays some inductions.

choices: I will cohort all admissions with rapid antigen plus mandatory masking and HEPA in postpartum bays, accepting some false-negative transmission risk under saturation.
I will halt non-emergent inductions until PCR clears or portable negative-pressure pods arrive tomorrow, accepting preeclampsia progression risk for scheduled patients tonight.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Labor and delivery is at one hundred ten percent capacity with six inductions waiting. Casey Marsh must choose between antigen cohorting or halting non-emergent inductions for PCR. What does Casey Marsh do?
